title: Contributing to zzdataframe2graphic
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("rgt47/zzdataframe2graphic", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Advanced Features: zzobj2fig
code: library(zzobj2fig) #> zzobj2fig 0.3.0 - LaTeX table generation for R

title: Advanced Features: zzobj2fig
text: Professional tables often require footnotes explain abbreviations, provide context, indicate significance levels. zzobj2fig provides full footnote support via o2f_footnote() function.

title: Advanced Features: zzobj2fig
code: results <- data.frame(   Variable = c("Age", "BMI", "Systolic BP", "HbA1c"),   Treatment = c("52.3 (8.2)", "28.1 (4.3)", "142.5 (18.3)", "7.2 (1.1)"),   Control = c("51.8 (7.9)", "27.9 (4.1)", "140.2 (17.1)", "7.4 (1.2)"),   `P-value` = c("0.542", "0.631", "0.048", "0.089"),   check.names = FALSE )  fn <- o2f_footnote(   general = c(     "Values are mean (SD) unless otherwise noted.",     "BMI = body mass index; BP = blood pressure; HbA1c = hemoglobin A1c."   ) )  o2f(results,     filename = "baseline_characteristics",     sub_dir = output_dir,     caption = "Baseline Characteristics by Treatment Group",     footnote = fn,     theme = "nejm")

title: Advanced Features: zzobj2fig
text: APA format requires specific footnote organization: general notes, specific notes, probability notes.
code: anova_results <- data.frame(   Source = c("Treatment", "Time", "Treatment x Time", "Error"),   SS = c(245.32, 189.45, 52.18, 1024.56),   df = c(2, 3, 6, 108),   MS = c(122.66, 63.15, 8.70, 9.49),   F = c(12.93, 6.66, 0.92, NA),   p = c("<0.001", "0.002", "0.485", NA) )  anova_results$F[1] <- o2f_mark("12.93", 1, "symbol") anova_results$F[2] <- o2f_mark("6.66", 2, "symbol")  fn_apa <- o2f_footnote(   general = "SS = sum of squares; MS = mean square.",   symbol = c("p < .001", "p < .01"),   title_general = "Note.",   title_symbol = NULL,   footnote_as_chunk = TRUE )  o2f(anova_results,     filename = "anova_table",     sub_dir = output_dir,     caption = "Analysis of Variance Results",     footnote = fn_apa,     theme = "apa")

title: Advanced Features: zzobj2fig
code: endpoints <- data.frame(   Endpoint = c("Primary: ADAS-Cog14", "Secondary: CDR-SB",                "Secondary: ADCS-ADL", "Exploratory: Brain Volume"),   Difference = c("-0.45", "-0.21", "0.18", "-2.1%"),   CI = c("(-0.82, -0.08)", "(-0.39, -0.03)", "(-0.15, 0.51)",          "(-3.8%, -0.4%)"),   P = c("0.018", "0.024", "0.285", "0.016") )  endpoints$Endpoint[1] <- o2f_mark(endpoints$Endpoint[1], 1, "number") endpoints$Difference[4] <- o2f_mark(endpoints$Difference[4], 2, "number")  fn_numbered <- o2f_footnote(   number = c(     "Co-primary endpoint with CDR-SB.",     "Percentage change from baseline in whole brain volume."   ) )  o2f(endpoints,     filename = "endpoints_table",     sub_dir = output_dir,     caption = "Efficacy Endpoints at Week 78",     footnote = fn_numbered,     theme = "nejm")

title: Advanced Features: zzobj2fig
text: Spanning headers group related columns common label, essential complex tables comparing multiple conditions timepoints.

title: Advanced Features: zzobj2fig
code: comparison <- data.frame(   Measure = c("Score A", "Score B", "Score C"),   T_Mean = c(23.4, 18.7, 42.1),   T_SD = c(5.2, 3.8, 8.9),   C_Mean = c(21.2, 19.1, 38.5),   C_SD = c(4.9, 4.1, 9.2) ) names(comparison) <- c("Measure", "Mean", "SD", "Mean", "SD")  hdr <- o2f_header_above(   " " = 1,   "Treatment" = 2,    "Control" = 2 )  o2f(comparison,     filename = "treatment_comparison",     sub_dir = output_dir,     caption = "Treatment vs Control Outcomes",     header_above = hdr)

title: Advanced Features: zzobj2fig
code: longitudinal <- data.frame(   Variable = c("Cognitive", "Functional", "Behavioral"),   W12_T = c(-1.2, -0.8, -0.3),   W12_C = c(-0.9, -0.6, -0.2),   W24_T = c(-2.1, -1.5, -0.5),   W24_C = c(-1.4, -1.0, -0.3),   W52_T = c(-3.8, -2.8, -0.9),   W52_C = c(-2.2, -1.8, -0.5) ) names(longitudinal) <- c("Domain", "Trt", "Ctrl", "Trt", "Ctrl", "Trt", "Ctrl")  headers <- list(   o2f_header_above(     " " = 1,     "Week 12" = 2,     "Week 24" = 2,     "Week 52" = 2   ),   o2f_header_above(     " " = 1,     "Change from Baseline" = 6,     line = FALSE   ) )  o2f(longitudinal,     filename = "longitudinal_outcomes",     sub_dir = output_dir,     caption = "Change from Baseline by Timepoint",     header_above = headers,     theme = "nature")

title: Advanced Features: zzobj2fig
text: data hierarchical structure, collapsing repeated values multi-row cells improves readability.

title: Advanced Features: zzobj2fig
code: subgroup <- data.frame(   Category = c("Age", "Age", "Sex", "Sex", "Race", "Race", "Race"),   Subgroup = c("<65", ">=65", "Male", "Female", "White", "Black", "Asian"),   N = c(245, 189, 218, 216, 312, 67, 55),   Effect = c(0.42, 0.38, 0.41, 0.39, 0.40, 0.44, 0.36),   CI = c("(0.28, 0.56)", "(0.22, 0.54)", "(0.27, 0.55)", "(0.24, 0.54)",          "(0.28, 0.52)", "(0.24, 0.64)", "(0.14, 0.58)") )  o2f(subgroup,     filename = "subgroup_analysis",     sub_dir = output_dir,     caption = "Subgroup Analysis of Treatment Effect",     collapse_rows = o2f_collapse_rows(       columns = 1,       valign = "top",       latex_hline = "major"     ))

title: Advanced Features: zzobj2fig
code: adverse_events <- data.frame(   System = c(rep("Nervous System", 3), rep("Gastrointestinal", 3),              rep("General", 2)),   Category = c("Headache", "Dizziness", "Somnolence",                "Nausea", "Diarrhea", "Vomiting",                "Fatigue", "Pyrexia"),   Treatment = c(45, 32, 18, 28, 22, 15, 38, 12),   Placebo = c(41, 28, 14, 25, 19, 12, 35, 10) )  o2f(adverse_events,     filename = "adverse_events",     sub_dir = output_dir,     caption = "Adverse Events by System Organ Class",     collapse_rows = o2f_collapse_rows(       columns = 1,       valign = "middle",       latex_hline = "major"     ),     theme = "nejm")

title: Advanced Features: zzobj2fig
text: Proper decimal alignment makes numeric tables easier read compare. zzobj2fig provides siunitx integration precise alignment.

title: Advanced Features: zzobj2fig
code: stats <- data.frame(   Parameter = c("Mean", "Median", "SD", "Min", "Max"),   Treatment = c(127.45, 125.00, 18.234, 89.1, 178.92),   Control = c(124.82, 123.50, 17.891, 91.3, 172.45) )  o2f(stats,     filename = "summary_stats",     sub_dir = output_dir,     caption = "Summary Statistics by Group",     align = list(       "l",       o2f_decimal(3, 2),       o2f_decimal(3, 2)     ))

title: Advanced Features: zzobj2fig

code: clinical <- data.frame(   Endpoint = c("ADAS-Cog14", "ADAS-Cog14", "CDR-SB", "CDR-SB"),   Timepoint = c("Week 52", "Week 78", "Week 52", "Week 78"),   N_T = c(423, 398, 423, 398),   N_C = c(421, 395, 421, 395),   Diff = c(-0.31, -0.45, -0.15, -0.21),   CI_Low = c(-0.58, -0.82, -0.28, -0.39),   CI_High = c(-0.04, -0.08, -0.02, -0.03),   P = c("0.024", "0.018", "0.025", "0.024") )  clinical$Diff <- sapply(seq_len(nrow(clinical)), function(i) {   if (as.numeric(clinical$P[i]) < 0.05) {     o2f_mark(as.character(clinical$Diff[i]), 1, "symbol")   } else {     as.character(clinical$Diff[i])   } })  hdr <- o2f_header_above(   " " = 2,   "N" = 2,   "Treatment Effect" = 4 )  fn <- o2f_footnote(   general = "Difference is treatment minus placebo (negative favors treatment).",   symbol = "p < 0.05 vs placebo.",   title_general = "Note:" )  o2f(clinical,     filename = "clinical_efficacy",     sub_dir = output_dir,     caption = "Efficacy Outcomes in the Modified ITT Population",     caption_short = "Efficacy Outcomes",     label = "tab:efficacy",     header_above = hdr,     footnote = fn,     collapse_rows = o2f_collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "top"),     theme = "nejm")

title: Why zzobj2fig? A Practical Comparison with pander
text: R provides multiple packages creating tables data frames, distinct design philosophies. vignette compares pander zzobj2fig practical examples help choose right tool. pander designed quick, inline table generation R Markdown documents. converts R objects Markdown format, Pandoc renders HTML, PDF, Word. approach fast integrates seamlessly R Markdown ecosystem. zzobj2fig takes different approach: generates standalone PDF tables via LaTeX compilation, crops inclusion documents. produces publication-quality tables features Markdown express, footnotes, spanning headers, decimal alignment. choice depends needs: Quick reports HTML output: pander simpler faster Journal submissions formal publications: zzobj2fig provides control formatting features required
code: suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(zzobj2fig))

code: df <- data.frame(   Endpoint = c("Primary", "Primary", "Secondary", "Secondary"),   Timepoint = c("Week 26", "Week 52", "Week 26", "Week 52"),   N = c(245, 232, 245, 232),   Difference = c(-0.42, -0.58, -0.28, -0.35),   P = c("0.008", "0.002", "0.045", "0.018") )  df$Difference <- sapply(seq_len(nrow(df)), function(i) {   p <- as.numeric(df$P[i])   if (p < 0.01) o2f_mark(as.character(df$Difference[i]), 2, "symbol")   else if (p < 0.05) o2f_mark(as.character(df$Difference[i]), 1, "symbol")   else as.character(df$Difference[i]) })  hdr <- o2f_header_above(" " = 2, "Results" = 3)  fn <- o2f_footnote(   general = "Negative values favor treatment.",   symbol = c("p < 0.05", "p < 0.01") )  o2f(df,     filename = "complex_table",     sub_dir = output_dir,     caption = "Efficacy Results by Endpoint and Timepoint",     caption_short = "Efficacy Results",     header_above = hdr,     collapse_rows = o2f_collapse_rows(1, valign = "top"),     footnote = fn,     theme = "nejm")

title: Quickstart Guide: zzobj2fig v0.2.1
text: wrap paragraph text around table, use wrapfig LaTeX package. First, add \usepackage{wrapfig} YAML header, use inline R code knitr::asis_output(): text flows around table right. position parameter controls placement: “r” right, “l” left. width parameter sets much horizontal space table occupies. Text continues wrap naturally sufficient content fills space beside table. approach works well small summary tables complement narrative without interrupting reading flow. Notice paragraph continues alongside table, making efficient use page space. text extends beyond table height, returns full width automatically. technique particularly useful research papers want discuss results displaying supporting data close proximity discussion. Additional text can continue demonstrate normal formatting resumes wrapped section.
code: wrap_table <- function(filename, position = "r", width = "0.45\\textwidth") {   pdf_path <- paste0(filename, "_cropped.pdf")   knitr::asis_output(sprintf("\\begin{wrapfigure}{%s}{%s}   \\centering   \\includegraphics{%s} \\end{wrapfigure}", position, width, pdf_path)) }

title: Quickstart Guide: zzo2f (Typst Backend)
text: Formatting specs consumed zzo2f() via formats parameter, accepts single spec list specs. zzo2f_format() general constructor; five thin wrappers common cases.
code: library(zzobj2fig)  df <- mtcars[1:4, 1:4]  zzo2f(   df,   filename = 'styled_demo',   formats = list(     zzo2f_bold_col('mpg'),     zzo2f_italic_col('hp'),     zzo2f_color_row(c(1, 3), background = 'gray!10'),     zzo2f_highlight(       function(x) as.numeric(x) > 20,       background = 'yellow!30'     ),     zzo2f_decimal(c('mpg', 'disp'), digits = 1)   ) )

title: Quickstart Guide: zzo2f (Typst Backend)
text: zzo2f_collapse_rows() visually merges consecutive identical values specified columns; zzo2f_header_above() delegates o2f_header_above() consumed via header_above.
code: df <- data.frame(   Group    = rep(c('A', 'B'), each = 3),   Subgroup = rep(c('x', 'y', 'z'), 2),   Value    = 1:6 )  zzo2f(   df,   filename = 'collapsed',   collapse_rows = zzo2f_collapse_rows('Group'),   header_above = zzo2f_header_above(     ' ' = 1, 'Identifier' = 2   ) )

title: Quickstart Guide: zzo2f (Typst Backend)
text: zzo2f_footnote() alias o2f_footnote(); spec backend-agnostic. zzo2f_mark() inserts Typst superscript markers; zzo2f_ref() returns Typst cross-reference string (@label).
code: df <- mtcars[1:3, 1:3] df$mpg[1] <- zzo2f_mark(as.character(df$mpg[1]), 1, 'symbol')  zzo2f(   df,   filename = 'fn_demo',   footnote = zzo2f_footnote(     general = 'Source: mtcars (Motor Trend, 1974).',     symbol = c('Leading observation.')   ) )  ref_str <- zzo2f_ref('tab-fn-demo', cat = FALSE)

title: Quickstart Guide: zzo2f (Typst Backend)

title: Quickstart Guide: zzo2f (Typst Backend)
text: zzo2f_include*() helpers emit Typst source embedding previously rendered PDF host .typ document. invoked side effect (print--return) R Markdown chunks, captured via cat = FALSE. Note Typst refuses paths outside project root, place host .typ file directory () referenced PDFs.
code: zzo2f(mtcars[1:3, 1:3], filename = 'inner')  zzo2f_include(   'inner',   caption = 'Demo embedded table.',   label = 'tab-inner',   cat = FALSE ) #> "#align(center)[#figure(image(\"inner.pdf\", width: 100%), ..."  # Side-by-side zzo2f_include_sidebyside(   'fig/a', 'fig/b',   main_caption = 'Comparison',   cat = FALSE )

title: Quickstart Guide: zzo2f (Typst Backend)

code: src <- zzo2f(   mtcars[1:3, 1:3],   filename = 'raw_demo',   compile = FALSE ) # src is a character vector of .typ lines with attributes # recording filename / sub_dir / format.  src <- c('// Added by hand', src) zzo2f_compile(src, filename = 'raw_demo')

title: Creating LaTeX Tables and Cropped PDFs from Dataframes
text: x: Object convert (data frame, matrix, lm, glm, etc.) filename: Base name output files. Defaults object name. sub_dir: Output subdirectory. Default: "output". scolor: LaTeX color row shading (e.g., "blue!10"). Default: "blue!10". verbose: Print progress messages. Default: FALSE. extra_packages: List LaTeX package specifications. document_class: LaTeX document class. Default: "article".

title: Creating LaTeX Tables and Cropped PDFs from Dataframes
text: caption: LaTeX table caption. caption_short: Short caption List Tables. label: LaTeX label cross-referencing. align: Column alignment vector list (supports siunitx). longtable: Enable multi-page tables. Default: FALSE. crop: Crop PDF output. Default: TRUE. crop_margin: Margin size points. Default: 10. theme: Theme name ("nejm", "apa", "nature", "minimal") o2f_theme object. footnote: o2f_footnote object table footnotes. header_above: o2f_header_above object spanning column headers. collapse_rows: o2f_collapse_rows object multi-row cells.

title: zzobj2fig
text: zzobj2fig R package creating LaTeX tables data frames statistical objects, generating cropped PDF outputs suitable direct inclusion publications. package provides journal-specific themes (APA, Nature, NEJM), S3 methods common statistical objects, R Markdown integration.

title: zzobj2fig
text: Output files generated: my_table.tex - LaTeX source my_table.pdf - Full PDF my_table_cropped.pdf - Cropped PDF document inclusion
code: library(zzobj2fig)  # Basic usage: data frame to cropped PDF o2f(mtcars[1:6, 1:4], filename = "my_table")  # With journal theme o2f(mtcars[1:6, 1:4], theme = "nejm", filename = "nejm_table")  # Statistical objects (lm, glm, anova, etc.) model <- lm(mpg ~ cyl + hp + wt, data = mtcars) o2f(model, filename = "regression_table")  # Model comparison m1 <- lm(mpg ~ cyl, data = mtcars) m2 <- lm(mpg ~ cyl + hp, data = mtcars) o2f_regression(Model1 = m1, Model2 = m2, stars = TRUE)

title: zzobj2fig
text: Built-themes common publication styles:
code: o2f(data, theme = "nejm")     # New England Journal of Medicine o2f(data, theme = "apa")      # American Psychological Association o2f(data, theme = "nature")   # Nature journals o2f(data, theme = "minimal")  # Clean, minimal style  # Set global theme o2f_theme_set("nejm")

title: zzobj2fig
text: Direct conversion statistical objects formatted tables: RMS package support (biostatistics/clinical trials): Additional types via broom integration: coxph, survreg, nls, polr, prcomp, kmeans, lmerMod, glmerMod, lme, Arima. broom-supported object works via o2f_tidy():
code: o2f_tidy(any_broom_object, filename = "result")

title: zzobj2fig
text: Inline tables without LaTeX float positioning issues:
code: # In R Markdown o2f_inline(model, width = "3in", caption = "Model Results")  # Quick coefficient table o2f_coef(model, caption = "Coefficients")

what: Typst Backend for Table-to-Figure Conversion — zzo2f
title: Typst Backend for Table-to-Figure Conversion — zzo2f
text: Convert data frames publication-quality table images using tinytable Typst CLI. provides lightweight alternative LaTeX-based o2f pipeline, requiring Typst (~40 MB binary) instead full LaTeX distribution. Generates PDF, PNG, SVG table images data frames using tinytable package Typst CLI. Typst auto-sizes pages, cropping step needed.

title: Typst Backend for Table-to-Figure Conversion — zzo2f
code: zzo2f(x, ...)

title: Typst Backend for Table-to-Figure Conversion — zzo2f
text: x data frame, matrix, table object render. ... Additional arguments passed tinytable::tt(). filename Character string. Base name output file (without extension). Defaults deparsed name x. sub_dir Character string. Output directory. Defaults "analysis/figures" zzcollab projects, "figures" otherwise. verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default FALSE. caption Character string NULL. Table caption. align Character vector NULL. Column alignments ("l", "c", "r"). Length 1 (applied columns) one per column. NULL auto-detect. theme Character string, o2f_theme object, NULL. Theme name (e.g., "nejm", "apa") custom theme. Uses existing o2f theme system. scolor Character string NULL. LaTeX color spec row striping, translated hex. Overrides theme setting. footnote o2f_footnote object NULL. Table footnotes. header_above o2f_header object, list o2f_header objects, NULL. Spanning column headers. format Character string. Output format: "pdf" (default), "png", "svg". dpi Integer. PNG resolution dots per inch. Default 300.

title: Typst Backend for Table-to-Figure Conversion — zzo2f
text: Invisibly returns path output file.

title: Typst Backend for Table-to-Figure Conversion — zzo2f
text: pipeline: Validate inputs coerce data.frame Resolve theme via existing o2f theme system, translate Typst Build tinytable object tt() / style_tt() / group_tt() Save .typ via save_tt() Compile via typst compile Parameters o2f() dropped (LaTeX-specific): extra_packages, document_class, caption_short, label, longtable, crop, crop_margin, collapse_rows.
